Tell Your Brand Story with AI: Generate Promotional Visuals with Warmth and Emotion

One story-rich brand image can communicate more than a thousand words. It carries your brand’s tone, builds emotional resonance, and helps audiences remember what you stand for. In this tutorial, we use Qwen text-to-image to generate a warm bakery-themed storytelling visual, then break down the complete parameters and creative process.

I. Our Goal: Warm, Real, Narrative-Driven

We want a promotional image suitable for brand storytelling, with these core qualities:

  • Narrative power: the image itself tells a story.
  • Photorealistic feel: close to real photography, not “obviously AI.”
  • Warm atmosphere: handcrafted, authentic, and human.
  • Rich details: enough visual layers for viewers to explore.

II. Core Parameters (Copy & Paste Ready)

{
  "prompt": "A cozy small artisan bakery at early morning. A middle-aged baker in a white apron is taking freshly baked sourdough bread out of a traditional stone oven. Warm golden sunlight enters through the window, illuminating floating flour particles in the air. Interior style is rustic, with wooden shelves filled with assorted bread. Outside the window, the rising sun lights a cobblestone street. The overall mood conveys handmade craft, warmth, and authenticity. Realistic photography style, warm tones, shallow depth of field.",
  "image_size": "landscape_4_3",
  "num_inference_steps": 35,
  "guidance_scale": 7.0,
  "enable_safety_checker": true,
  "output_format": "png",
  "negative_prompt": "text, watermark, logo, modern appliances, plastic products, cold color tone, over-sharpening, deformed people, blurry",
  "acceleration": "regular"
}

Parameter Breakdown

Parameter Setting Purpose
image_size landscape_4_3 Classic ratio suitable for websites and brochures.
guidance_scale 7.0 Medium prompt adherence with room for creative variation.
negative_prompt Exclude modern/irrelevant elements Maintains a handcrafted rustic visual identity.
num_inference_steps 35 Balances generation speed and visual detail.

IV. How to Customize Your Own Brand Story Visuals

Step 1: Define the Core Brand Emotion

Brand Tone Prompt Keywords
Warm / healing warm light, sunlight, wooden textures, handmade, cozy, authentic
Professional / rigorous clean, bright, sharp, modern, minimal
Creative / energetic vibrant colors, dynamic movement, inspiration, youthful energy
Vintage / nostalgic film look, old-time mood, warm palette, classic era details

Step 2: Choose Scene + Character + Action

  • Scenes: bakery, cafe, studio, outdoors, home environment.
  • Characters: baker, barista, creator, family members, friends.
  • Actions: crafting, talking, creating, serving, interacting.

Step 3: Control Lighting and Mood

  • Natural light: “morning sunlight through windows.”
  • Warm key light: “soft golden light.”
  • Atmosphere light: “candle glow,” “neon ambient lighting.”

V. Advanced Tips for More Emotional Brand Visuals

  • Shallow depth of field: add “background blur” to focus attention on the subject.
  • Detail drives realism: include cues like flour dust, steam, latte art, fabric texture.
  • Define expressions: “focused eyes,” “natural smile,” “calm concentration.”
  • Build a visual series: generate multiple images with consistent style for brand identity.
  • Reserve layout space: add “empty area on the left/right” for slogan or logo placement.

VI. Why Brand Story Visuals Matter

Value Point Explanation
Emotional connection Story-led visuals build trust and human connection with your audience.
Content reuse One image can be repurposed across websites, social media, ads, and print.
Brand recognition Consistent visual language strengthens memory and brand recall.
Social sharing Emotionally strong visuals are more likely to be saved and shared.

VII. Summary

The core of a brand storytelling image is not just product display—it is emotion and values. With Qwen text-to-image, you can precisely control scene, people, lighting, and atmosphere to create warm narrative visuals for campaigns. Use this setup as your brand visual template, then tweak prompts by brand tone to build a scalable visual system.
